feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day

entry.title: 'Our bus was going up in smoke': Jos Buttler reveals scary incident after Gujarat Titans' IPL 2026 final loss to RCB
entry.content: Gujarat Titans' Jos Buttler described a frightening safety scare after their IPL final loss. The team bus caught fire due to a short circuit, forcing players and staff to evacuate. This incident compounded their disappointment after falling short against Royal Challengers Bengaluru, adding a bizarre end to their campaign.

entry.title: ‘They haven’t got the message’ – Florentino Perez fires back at Real Madrid sale accusations from Enrique Riquelme
entry.content: Florentino Perez has publicly responded to one of the biggest talking points of the ongoing Real Madrid presidential election campaign, rejecting claims that he is preparing the club for a future sale...

entry.title: ☕️🥐 FC Breakfast: the transfer window heated up overnight!
entry.content: Transfer update: things moved overnight 🔥According to Fabrizio Romano, Denzel Dumfries is expected to join Real Madrid. Another departure in Italy, with Ederson signing for Manchester United for �...

entry.title: Two Spanish giants keen on Liverpool star
entry.content: Real Madrid and Atlético Madrid Eye Alexis Mac Allister TransferMadrid Interest Places Liverpool on AlertAlexis Mac Allister has become one of those players whose value is measured not only in assist...

entry.title: Morgan Rogers among ‘at least 10 names’ on Chelsea’s summer wishlist — report
entry.content: And if wishes were fishes…

entry.title: Aston Villa stand firm against Arsenal and other interest for star
entry.content: Morgan Rogers Transfer Interest: Aston Villa Face Arsenal Test After Breakout SeasonMorgan Rogers has become the sort of footballer who changes the temperature of a summer. Not because Aston Villa wan...

entry.title: Bayern Munich reach agreement with Ismaël Saibari
entry.content: Bayern Munich have reached an agreement with PSV Eindhoven attacker Ismaël Saibari (25), according to a report by Foot Mercato.Saibari, who scored 15 goals and provided eight assists in 27 Eredivisie...

entry.title: Bears have no choice but to make this trade after Myles Garrett deal with Rams
entry.content: The Bears have to keep up.

entry.title: 🚨 €45m deal! Manchester United seal summer transfer
entry.content: This transfer was a tough piece of work for Manchester United. But now the Red Devils have agreed a deal with Atalanta Bergamo and Éderson. That is being reported by both 'The Athletic', as well as t...

entry.title: NBA Insider Reveals ‘Strong’ Connection Between Knicks and Giannis Antetokounmpo And How It Will Play Out Come Next Season
entry.content: The New York Knicks were among the most heavily-linked to Giannis Antetokounmpo throughout the season. Neither the Greek Freak nor his current team the Milwaukee Bucks confirmed it but rumors lived on. Shams Charania is among the reporters relentless in reporting about Antetokounmpo’s move away from Wisconsin and in his latest scoop, he revealed that [...]

entry.title: One factor not expected to be considered in Vikings' decision to start J.J. McCarthy and Kyler Murray
entry.content: What could decide who starts?

entry.title: Celtics now viewed as free agent suitor for only player who has played well against Knicks in NBA Playoffs
entry.content: Could Boston land this FA?

entry.title: Chelsea set sights on Serie A star who Xabi Alonso wanted at Real Madrid
entry.content: Chelsea are reportedly interested in signing Juventus left-back Andrea Cambiaso.The Blues endured a challenging 2025-26 campaign, with both Enzo Maresca and Liam Rosenior losing their jobs after poor ...

entry.title: Roma reject Chelsea’s €50 million bid for Svilar
entry.content: Mile Svilar isn’t leaving anytime soon. This is the message Roma sent to Chelsea after the English club attempted to open negotiations for the Giallorossi goalkeeper. A clear response, with no room ...

entry.title: 🛫 Ceballos, heading for the exit: Míchel wants him at Ajax
entry.content: Dani Ceballos’ future appears to be moving further and further away from the Bernabéu. As Fabrizio Romano has reported, Ajax have formally opened talks to secure the services of the midfielder from...

entry.title: Tarik Subal urged to show ‘self-respect’ in potential Dodgers trade, but fans need to get real
entry.content: What are fans talking about?

entry.title: U.S., Iran Trade Heavy Fire in Persian Gulf, Testing Fragile Ceasefire
entry.content: The fresh fighting was the most intense in months and came as diplomatic efforts have stalled.

entry.title: Oil Prices Rise Amid Continuing Middle East Tensions
entry.content: An exchange of attacks between the U.S. and Iran could complicate negotiations and put at risk the reopening the Strait of Hormuz, a critical oil waterway.

entry.title: See How the Middle East’s Ceasefires Remain Plagued by Violence
entry.content: In Gaza, Lebanon and the Persian Gulf, strikes, skirmishes and military operations keep the region on edge.

entry.title: Spurs vs. Knicks NBA Finals series preview with Tim Legler & Jared Weiss
entry.content: Kevin O’Connor previews the NBA Finals matchup between the San Antonio Spurs & the New York Knicks.

entry.title: Knicks vs. Spurs predictions: Who wins the 2026 NBA Finals? Here are our championship picks
entry.content: Our writers weigh in on the must-see Finals matchup between New York and San Antonio.

entry.title: MHSAA boys volleyball: Northville sweeps Hartland for semifinal berth
entry.content: It wasn't easy, but Northville advanced to the semifinals in the first season of MHSAA-sponsored boys volleyball with a sweep of Hartland.

entry.title: MHSAA boys volleyball: Northville sweeps Hartland for semifinal berth
entry.content: It wasn't easy, but Northville advanced to the semifinals in the first season of MHSAA-sponsored boys volleyball with a sweep of Hartland.

entry.title: Kiki Iriafen has another double-double and the Mystics beat the Sky 90-72
entry.content: Kiki Iriafen has another double-double and the Mystics beat the Sky 90-72

entry.title: Red Sox 2025 Draft Pick Dominating On Mound Since Portland Promotion
entry.content: Boston Red Sox pitching prospect Anthony Eyanson has fared well in Double-A.

entry.title: Tennessee’s 2027 pitching rotation had a big day on Tuesday
entry.content: The process of building a better roster for 2027 for the Tennessee Vols is now underway for Josh Elander and his staff. With the transfer portal now open, the work begins to land some difference makers for next season. However, the portal works both ways, and it’s a matter of being able to keep your […]

entry.title: Scott Pelley fired from '60 Minutes' after accusing CBS News bosses of 'murdering' the program
entry.content: The veteran correspondent is leaving following a dispute with his bosses at CBS' '60 Minutes.'

entry.title: Egypt to hold open training session for 'limited' number of fans in Spokane ahead of World Cup
entry.content: Jun. 2—Some lucky soccer fans in the Inland Northwest will have the chance to see Egyptian national team and superstar Mohamed Salah in person ahead of the 2026 World Cup. But with limited supply and a high demand, they face long odds. The Egyptian National Soccer team will host an open training session at Luger Field on the Gonzaga University campus at noon June 10. The open session will take ...

entry.title: Man who confessed to killing girlfriend’s baby, throwing body in pond executed
entry.content: A man who confessed to killing his girlfriend’s baby and throwing the body in a pond has been executed in Florida

entry.title: Scientists charged with bringing deactivated mpox virus into US, lying to authorities
entry.content: Two scientists at a U.S. government lab have been charged with smuggling vials of deactivated mpox virus into the country from Africa without permits and lying about it to investigators

entry.title: Fed Chair Warsh makes first hires at central bank, including ‘Project 2025’ author
entry.content: The new Fed chair has made his first hires. One wrote the Fed chapter in conservative policy blueprint "Project 2025."

entry.title: Student Murder Sparks Accusations U.K. Police Are Biased Against White Victims
entry.content: A call to police claimed a man had been racially abused. In fact, the man was a killer with a cover story.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
Don’t worry about money. The best things in life are free.
Lucky Numbers: 2, 36, 81, 36, 68, 33

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Put a paper towel under your fruit and vegetables in the fridge.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: The Simpsons is the longest running animated series on TV.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Sathya Sai Baba
entry.content: “You are always your own best guru, your own best teacher, the answers are always inside you.” — Sathya Sai Baba

entry.title: 'How I Met Your Mother' actor Nick Pasqual gets 32 years to life after brutally stabbing ex
entry.content: Nick Pasqual, the actor who appeared in 'How I Met Your Mother' and was convicted of trying to murder his estranged girlfriend in 2024, was sentenced to 32 years to life on Tuesday.

entry.title: Peabo Bryson, R&B singer known for Disney classics, dies at 75
entry.content: R&B singer Peabo Bryson dies Tuesday in Marietta, Ga., after suffering a stroke over the weekend, according to his familiy.

entry.title: GPs in England too ‘overloaded’ to help older people at risk of falling, say MPs
entry.content: NHS bosses giving evidence to public accounts committee admit current position is unacceptableGPs in England are so “overloaded” that they cannot help older people who are at risk of falling in what NHS bosses accept is an unacceptable failure of care, the House of Commons’ public accounts committee has said.Pressure on GPs’ time has intensified as a result of the government’s decision to give patients online access to their services, according to a report by the influential cross-party group of MPs. Continue reading...

entry.title: Control Resonant will bend your reality on September 24, 2026
entry.content: You can pre-order the Faden siblings' next adventure now.

entry.title: Silent Hill: Townfall will hit PC and PS5 on September 24
entry.content: Screen Burn is bringing Konami-branded horror to a fictional Scottish island.

entry.title: The Things We Do's Vanessa Lee Says You Can Skip an Upper Bleph in Your 30s and 40s: Here's What to Do Instead
entry.content: The Things We Do founder Vanessa Lee tells ET why you should skip an upper bleph in your 30s and 40s and the treatments to do instead.

entry.title: Insomniac Games' Wolverine looks comically violent
entry.content: The first gameplay trailer shows off bloody melee combat.

entry.title: Heat, bugs, germs: U.S. public health prepares for the World Cup
entry.content: For months ahead of the World Cup, states and cities have been preparing to protect travelers and local communities from potential threats including foodborne illness and infectious diseases.

entry.title: Florida Lawmakers Add Property-Tax Cut to November Ballot
entry.content: Supporters, like Gov. DeSantis, say property-tax revenue has far outpaced inflation in recent years. Detractors warn of trimmed services.

entry.title: Is bovine colostrum really 'liquid gold' for gut health?
entry.content: Wellness influencers are promoting supplements of cow colostrum, the first milk that mammals produce after giving birth, for advancing immunity and a healthy gut. Does science back up their advice?

entry.title: Federal court hears arguments over efforts to halt Trump's mail-in executive order
entry.content: A federal judge has heard from voting rights groups and a coalition of two dozen states that want the courts to halt President Donald Trump’s executive order creating a federal voter list and limiting mail voting, The plaintiffs argued in two lawsuits ...

entry.title: Microsoft reveals new quantum chip made with AI, says it will have systems by 2029
entry.content: Microsoft reveals new quantum chip made with AI, says it will have systems by 2029

entry.title: Android will now warn you if a caller is impersonating someone you know
entry.content: Google Photos and Google Play Books are also getting new AI-powered updates.

entry.title: The European Parliament may be ditching Google as its default search engine
entry.content: The EU's moves towards digital sovereignty might include its Parliament ditching Google.

entry.title: Trump signs an executive order to vet top AI models for national security risks
entry.content: President Donald Trump has signed a new executive order on artificial intelligence, less than two weeks after postponing a White House ceremony over his concerns that a similar policy could dull America’s edge on AI technology

entry.title: Trump signs scaled-back AI cybersecurity order
entry.content: AI models can voluntarily submitted to the government for review 30 days before release under Trump's new executive order.

entry.title: 5 ways to reduce everyday exposure to 'forever chemicals'
entry.content: Mara Hoplamazian has spent years reporting on 'forever chemicals,' or PFAS. Here's what they've learned about what may help limit everyday exposure to the contaminant.

entry.title: The One Thing All New Yorkers Like About Mamdani: His Love of the Knicks
entry.content: The Democratic socialist mayor, who has divided the city in recent weeks on taxes and Israel, has found a theme everyone can rally behind.

entry.title: Powerful Iran-backed militias in Iraq say they will start handing in weapons to authorities
entry.content: Powerful Iran-backed militias in Iraq say they will start handing in weapons to authorities

entry.title: Blue Origin says rocket explosion spared fuel tanks and key launch pad parts
entry.content: Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin says last week's rocket explosion spared fuel tanks and some other critical parts of the launch pad

entry.title: Meta tests ‘Series’ for episodic Reels on Instagram and Facebook
entry.content: Meta told TechCrunch that it's considering ways to monetize the new feature, but didn't share specifics on what that could look like.

entry.title: ‘We don’t have another country to run to’: Kenyans fear US plan for Ebola quarantine site
entry.content: People from town of potential site for US citizens with Ebola symptoms say it puts them at risk in country with no known casesPeople from a town in central Kenya where the US wants to set up an Ebola quarantine facility for its citizens have strongly criticised the plan, saying they fear it will expose them to the virus and that it is indicative of double standards on the part of the US.“Everybody should be quarantined in their home country. We shouldn’t allow foreigners to bring us diseases,” said Charles Mathenge, a taxi driver who lives near Laikipia Air Base, the proposed site in Nanyuki, 120 miles from the capital, Nairobi. “Kenya is our country, and we should be careful with it.” Continue reading...

entry.title: X caters to creators with new ‘React with Video’ feature
entry.content: X will now let you 'react with video' to posts.

entry.title: Anthropic scales Claude Mythos to critical infrastructure in 15+ countries
entry.content: Anthropic is expanding Project Glasswing, its security vulnerability program, and access to Mythos to 150 organizations across 15 countries — targeting critical infrastructure in power, water, healthcare, and communications where a cyberattack could affect 100 million people.

entry.title: Doctors checked Biden just after Trump debate as Jill Biden feared he had a stroke
entry.content: The Biden administration previously said doctors examined the president "days" following the debate, not in the moments after. The former first lady revealed more details in her new book.

entry.title: Apple’s MacBook Neo is winning over a new generation of buyers
entry.content: The MacBook Neo shipped 1.1 million units in its first weeks on sale, IDC estimates, as Apple pushes deeper into the mainstream laptop market.

entry.title: The UK government has set a target of an 87% cut in carbon emissions by 2042
entry.content: The British government is sticking to its net-zero goal despite global conflicts affecting energy supplies

entry.title: Amazon Prime Day 2026 takes place June 23-26
entry.content: Groceries and household essentials will be a main focus of this year's deals event.

entry.title: Blue Origin plans to launch New Glenn again this year after explosion
entry.content: CEO Dave Limp said damage to the company's launchpad in Florida was not as bad as expected. But Blue Origin still hasn't shared the cause of last week's explosion.

entry.title: Florida sues OpenAI, CEO Sam Altman, claiming company concealed serious ChatGPT risks
entry.content: The state of Florida has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman, claiming the company knowingly released and aggressively marketed ChatGPT to the public while concealing serious risks

entry.title: The Star Fox remake is a test for the franchise's future
entry.content: Nintendo is trying to bring back Fox's mojo with a beautiful remake of the series' best game.

entry.title: How Ebola kills -- and what it takes to stop it
entry.content: It's a virus that can strike with unrelenting force. The kind of care need to knock it out is often not fully available in a lower resource country like the Democratic Republic of Congo.

entry.title: ZeroDrift raises $10M to protect AI models from themselves
entry.content: A new AI compliance service sits between AI models and end users to flag and replace any messages that might present a compliance problem.

entry.title: As Californians head to polls, these are the close races to watch
entry.content: In California, voters will head to the polls on Tuesday with tight contests playing out in the gubernatorial and L.A. mayoral races. Matt Gutman reports.

entry.title: Senior Iranian officer says return to war with U.S. seems "inevitable"
entry.content: Prospects for a U.S.-Iran deal seem to dim as the war between Israel and Hezbollah grinds on despite Trump saying they agreed to stop fighting.

entry.title: Eye Opener: White House pauses plans for $1.8 billion "anti-weaponization" fund
entry.content: The Trump administration pauses its plan for a nearly $1.8 billion "anti-weaponization" fund that critics called a slush fund for Trump supporters. Plus, tensions flare over a growing Ebola outbreak. All that and all that matters in today's Eye Opener.

entry.title: Russia’s Attacks on Civilians Grow More Vicious as It Stalls on Battlefield
entry.content: The heavy nationwide drone and missile strikes killed at least 18 people and injured over 100, according to Ukrainian authorities.

entry.title: Eye Opener: White House pauses plans for $1.8 billion "anti-weaponization" fund
entry.content: The Trump administration pauses its plan for a nearly $1.8 billion "anti-weaponization" fund that critics called a slush fund for Trump supporters. Plus, tensions flare over a growing Ebola outbreak. All that and all that matters in today's Eye Opener.

entry.title: Analysis-How a nudge from Nvidia propelled frugal Micron into the AI boom and a $1 trillion market cap
entry.content: Analysis-How a nudge from Nvidia propelled frugal Micron into the AI boom and a $1 trillion market cap

entry.title: Weight-loss drugs can cut breast cancer risk by up to 30%, studies suggest
entry.content: Three studies add to evidence that jabs could be part of cancer-fighting toolkit to cut risk of developing or dying from diseaseWeight-loss drugs can cut the risk of developing or dying from cancer by 30%, doctors have said.Millions of people already use the drugs to treat obesity. Now a series of studies presented at the world’s largest oncology conference suggest the drugs could play a role in preventing and treating cancer. Continue reading...

entry.title: Rocket engine startup Impulse raises $500 million to hire people, not AI
entry.content: Engineering physical systems still depends on human talent, according to Impulse Space president Eric Romo.

entry.title: Russian attack kills at least 18 in Ukraine, some of them children buried under rubble
entry.content: Russia launched hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles against Kyiv and other Ukrainian cities overnight, killing at least 18 civilians and wounding more than 100 others, authorities said Tuesday.

entry.title: Stocks rally on AI optimism; jitters over Iran simmer
entry.content: Stocks rally on AI optimism; jitters over Iran simmer

entry.title: 'Love my woke pope': Why Leo's first encyclical went viral and how it speaks to his papal approach
entry.content: Pope Leo XIV has issued a manifesto calling for robust regulation of artificial intelligence

entry.title: Trump-Netanyahu Tensions Flare Over Israel's Strikes on Lebanon
entry.content: Israel’s intensifying war in Lebanon has become the main sticking point blocking a peace deal with Iran. In a phone conversation with Israel’s Benjamin Netanyahu, President Donald Trump reportedly exploded on the call, cursing, calling him “crazy” and accusing the prime minister of “ingratitude.” NBC’s Richard Engel reports for TODAY.

entry.title: Bear goes on rampage in Japan, injuring 4 people
entry.content: A record 13 people were killed by bears in Japan last year, and there has been a jump in sightings as the animals emerge hungry from hibernation.

entry.title: Voters Head to Polls in Key States, Setting the Stage for Midterms
entry.content: Voters are gearing up for a key primaries with razor-tight races in six states, including Iowa where Democrats are hoping to win a Senate seat in the traditionally Republican state. Meanwhile in California, former reality star Spencer Pratt is gaining late momentum in his push to lead Los Angeles, the nation’s second-largest city. NBC’s Liz Kreutz reports for TODAY.

entry.title: Apparent hack targets Space Force official's Instagram account, posts pro-Iran content
entry.content: An apparent hack targeted a key U.S. Space Force official's Instagram account recently and reportedly posted pro-Iran and anti-American content.

entry.title: Trump to Drop $1.8B ‘Anti-Weaponization’ Fund Amid Pushback
entry.content: The Trump administration is appearing to back off plans for its $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization" fund after a judge’s order put it on hold. Democrats called it a “slush fund” for Trump allies but there was also unusually fierce opposition from Republicans with some even threatening to team up with Democrats to block it. NBC’s Gabe Gutierrez reports for TODAY.

entry.title: Private-Credit Firms’ Insurers Brace for Looming Legal Storm
entry.content: The insurers are preparing for legal fights against boards and executives over valuation practices.

entry.title: Pacific Fusion’s latest prototype packs 440 gigawatts into an 80-nanosecond burst
entry.content: Pacific Fusion's sub-scale prototype delivered enormous amounts of power in a flash, setting the company up for its demonstration power plant.

entry.title: China’s Chip Ambitions Run Into a Global Tech Wall
entry.content: Plus, Huawei will likely trail rivals by six to eight years by 2031 despite its innovations.

entry.title: Russian missile, drone attack on Ukraine kills at least 13, officials say
entry.content: Russian missile, drone strikes kill at least 13 people across Ukraine, authorities say, after President Zelenskyy warned Moscow was planning a "massive new strike."

entry.title: Ancient coin, mysterious markings found under Notre Dame cathedral
entry.content: A team of archaeologists at the iconic cathedral is digging straight down and back in time, to Roman Paris 2,000 years ago.

entry.title: Your FOMO Is Strong, but Pity Finance Pros
entry.content: Plus, Alphabet makes a cash call.

entry.title: Meet the Harvard grad who might lead the U.S. to World Cup glory
entry.content: Matt Freese once wrote a college research project on penalty kicks. Now he’s competing to be the starting goalie for the U.S. men’s national team at this summer’s World Cup.

entry.title: As Congo grapples with Ebola, volunteers cook up meals to support patients and health workers
entry.content: As Congo grapples with Ebola, volunteers cook up meals to support patients and health workers

entry.title: Why ‘Nvidia Inside’ Can Work in the PC Market
entry.content: The world’s only $5 trillion company can sell the concept that AI computing won’t be confined to data centers.

entry.title: Stock Market Today: Investors Weigh AI Fervor Against Mideast Worries
entry.content: Bond yields ease, oil slips after Trump pushes for pause in Israel-Hezbollah fighting

entry.title: Musket balls and a burnt hull: evidence of real pirates of the Caribbean found in Bahamas
entry.content: Exclusive: First shipwrecks found in Nassau harbour on New Providence, once the hideout of Blackbeard and Calico JackThe first shipwrecks linked to the real pirates of the Caribbean in the Bahamas have been discovered by an international team co-directed by a British marine archaeologist.Blackbeard and Calico Jack Rackham were among pirates who, between the 1690s and 1720s, turned Nassau on the island of New Providence into a hideout where they plotted their next heists on the high seas and divided up their plunder. Continue reading...

entry.title: Mozambique says five citizens killed in ‘xenophobic attacks’ in South Africa
entry.content: About 800 Mozambicans said to be caught up in violence in Mossel Bay as anti-immigration protests sweep countryFive Mozambique nationals were killed in “xenophobic attacks” in South Africa at the weekend, the Mozambican government said – the first deaths officially linked to protests against illegal immigration sweeping the country.About 800 Mozambican nationals were caught up in violence that broke out in the southern coastal city of Mossel Bay on Friday, a government statement said. Continue reading...

entry.title: Rubio to testify before Congress for the first time since the start of the Iran war
entry.content: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is set to face a litany of questions about the Trump administration’s fragile and stalling diplomatic efforts around the world when he appears for back-to-back hearings on Capitol Hill for the first time since the Iran wa...

entry.title: Republican senators want more answers on $1.8 billion settlement fund as Trump considers its future
entry.content: Senate Republicans are meeting to discuss next steps in a standoff with the White House

entry.title: Democrats redrew California's map to counter Trump. The primary tests whether it pays off for them
entry.content: California’s congressional primaries on Tuesday are a preliminary test of Democrats’ best chance at countering Republican redistricting gains elsewhere this year
